title: Elio and the Lost Baby Bird
synopsis: When Elio finds a scared baby bird that has fallen from its nest, he goes on a brave adventure through the park to help it find its way home, learning about kindness and courage along the way.

pages.text: Elio was playing in the sunny park with his mama. He felt happy running on the soft grass and watching the fluffy clouds. Suddenly, he heard a tiny sound. "Peep! Peep!" What could it be?

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with curly brown hair wearing a bright red t-shirt and blue shorts running happily in a green park. His Black mother with natural hair in a yellow dress watches nearby. Puffy white clouds float in a bright blue sky.
illustration.alt: Elio running happily in the park with his mama watching

pages.text: Elio looked down and saw a small baby bird sitting on the ground. The little bird looked scared and sad. Its tiny wings were shaking, and it kept saying "peep peep" in a worried voice.

illustration.description: A close-up view of a tiny yellow baby bird with big worried eyes sitting on green grass. The bird has small fluffy feathers and looks frightened. Elio's red shoes are visible at the edge of the image.
illustration.alt: A scared baby bird on the ground with Elio's feet nearby

pages.text: "Oh no, little bird!" said Elio gently. "Are you lost?" The baby bird looked up at Elio with big, round eyes. Elio felt worried too, but he wanted to be brave and help his new friend.

illustration.description: Elio kneeling down on the grass looking concerned at the baby bird. His expression is gentle and caring. The baby bird is looking up at him trustingly. Warm sunlight filters through green tree leaves above them.
illustration.alt: Elio kneeling down to talk to the worried baby bird

pages.text: Elio called to his mama. "Mama, can we help this baby bird find its family?" His mama smiled. "That's very kind, Elio. Let's look up in the trees for a nest." Elio felt excited to go on an adventure!

illustration.description: Elio pointing up at tall green trees while his mama bends down to look at the baby bird. Both have hopeful expressions. The trees have thick brown trunks and lush green leaves creating a canopy above.
illustration.alt: Elio and his mama looking up at trees to find the bird's nest

pages.text: They walked around the park, looking high and low. Elio saw a friendly Asian girl playing with her dad, and an elderly white man feeding ducks by the pond. Everyone was happy to help look for the nest, but they couldn't find it anywhere.

illustration.description: A diverse group of people in the park helping to search. An Asian girl in a pink dress points at trees while her father looks up. An elderly man with gray hair near a blue pond with white ducks. Everyone has helpful, friendly expressions.
illustration.alt: Different people in the park helping to search for the bird's nest

pages.text: Elio started to feel a little sad. Where could the baby bird's family be? The tiny bird was still scared and made soft peeping sounds. "Don't worry, little friend," Elio whispered. "I won't give up!"

illustration.description: Elio gently cupping the baby bird in his small hands, looking determined despite feeling worried. The baby bird nestles trustingly in his palms. Soft afternoon light creates a warm, gentle mood.
illustration.alt: Elio holding the baby bird carefully in his hands

pages.text: Then Elio heard something wonderful - "Tweet tweet!" It was coming from a big oak tree! A mama bird and papa bird were calling from their nest high up in the branches. They sounded worried and were looking for their baby!

illustration.description: Two adult yellow birds with orange beaks perched on a nest made of twigs in a large oak tree with thick green leaves. They look anxious and are calling downward. The nest sits securely in a fork of brown branches.
illustration.alt: Parent birds in their nest high up in an oak tree calling for their baby

pages.text: "We found your family!" Elio said happily. His mama carefully placed the baby bird in a low branch so it could hop up to its nest. The baby bird felt brave now and chirped with joy as it climbed higher and higher.

illustration.description: Elio's mama, a tall Black woman with gentle hands, carefully placing the baby bird on a low tree branch. The baby bird looks excited and determined, spreading its tiny wings. Elio watches with a big smile.
illustration.alt: Elio's mama helping place the baby bird on a tree branch

pages.text: Soon the baby bird was back safe in its cozy nest with its mama and papa. All three birds sang happy songs together. Elio felt proud and joyful. "We did it!" he cheered. Helping others made his heart feel warm and full.

illustration.description: The reunited bird family in their nest, all three birds cuddling together and singing with open beaks and joyful expressions. Below, Elio jumps with his arms raised in celebration while his mama claps proudly.
illustration.alt: The happy bird family reunited in their nest with Elio celebrating below

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did the baby bird feel when Elio first found it?
questions.explanation: The baby bird was scared and sad because it was lost and away from its family.

questions.question: What did Elio and his mama look for to help the baby bird?
questions.explanation: They looked up in the trees for the baby bird's nest where its family would be.

questions.question: How did Elio feel when he helped the baby bird find its family?
questions.explanation: Elio felt proud and joyful because helping others made his heart feel warm and full.

questions.question: Where did the parent birds live?
questions.explanation: The mama and papa bird lived in a nest high up in a big oak tree.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Choose the right word to complete each sentence from the story.

blanks.sentence: Elio found a _____ baby bird on the ground.
They looked up in the _____ for the bird's nest.
The baby bird felt _____ when it found its family.

pages.title: Feeling Words
pages.instructions: Match the feeling words to complete the sentences.

blanks.sentence: Elio felt _____ when he wanted to help the bird.
The parent birds felt _____ when they couldn't find their baby.
